label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, so Alice and Bob are playing this game with tokens. The rules are: they take turns removing either 1 or 4 tokens from a stack. Alice goes first, and whoever takes the last token wins. The question is asking for how many positive integers n (up to 2024) are such that Bob has a winning strategy, no matter what Alice does. First, I need to figure out for which values of n the starting player (Alice) can force a win, and for which values the second player (Bob) can force a win. Then, count how many of those n ≤ 2024 are in Bob's favor. This seems like a classic combinatorial game problem, probably solvable with dynamic programming or
